summary: Five mechanical edits across three test files: dropped one unused requireNonNull import, converted two empty `java.util.Map.of()` calls to `emptyMap()`, and replaced two `if (msg == null) "" else msg!!.lowercase()` ternaries with `msg?.lowercase().orEmpty()` on error-path assertions. Behaviour-preserving. The trino-main kit-pass already handled the bulk of test-side work incidentally during the @JvmRecord handle migration (~270 `.foo()` → `.foo` call-site updates across ~5 test files); that surface is not counted in this slice.

patterns_searched.java.util.{List,Map,Set}.{of,copyOf}: 8 sites across 3 files — 2 empty `Map.of()` converted to `emptyMap()`; 6 with-args `Map.of(k, v)` / `Set.of<T>(x)` KEPT per trino-main precedent (`kept_at: java.util.List.of(arg) (with arguments) — left as-is to avoid subtly changing the returned type's identity`)
patterns_searched.Objects.requireNonNull / requireNonNull(: 1 site — only an unused import in DucklakeTestCatalogEnvironment.kt:23 (the file uses `requireNotNull(...)` Kotlin stdlib; the Java static import was dead). Dropped.
patterns_searched.e.message!! / *.message!!: 2 sites in TestDucklakeArithmeticPushdownParity.kt:114,161 — both `if (thrown.message == null) "" else thrown.message!!.lowercase()` inside an error-path `assertThat(catchThrowable {...})`. Replaced with `thrown.message?.lowercase().orEmpty()`. Behaviour identical.
patterns_searched..toLowerCase() / .lowercase() (default-l: 3 sites — one in TestDucklakeDuckDbExecutorBackends.kt:92 reading `os.arch` (ASCII arch strings 'aarch64'/'x86_64'; not a catalog identifier — KEPT per brief rule #3). Two in TestDucklakeArithmeticPushdownParity (above) — those don't need Locale.ROOT (matching strings 'division by zero' etc. are ASCII assertion targets). Note: DucklakeTestCatalogEnvironment.kt:176 already uses `lowercase(Locale.ROOT)` — port pass was already correct.
patterns_searched.@JvmStatic occurrences: 259 occurrences across 42 .kt files — INTENTIONALLY KEPT per brief rule #1 (JUnit reflective loading + Trino test framework reflects on companions for @BeforeAll/@AfterAll/@MethodSource).
patterns_searched.Optional<T> call sites: many across test surface — DEFERRED per brief rule #7 + all prior slices.
patterns_searched.try { ... } finally { ... }: many sites across test files (e.g. TestDucklakeDuckDbReadMode 17 sites, TestDucklakeArithmeticPushdownParity 6 sites). All inspected are multi-step teardown (computeActual + tryDropTable, executor shutdown, etc.) NOT single-Closeable patterns — KEPT per brief rule #2.

notes: The ~270-callsite update during the trino-main @JvmRecord handle migration (commit edab5b4) touched ~5 trino-test files for `.foo()` → `.foo` accessor changes. Per brief, those incidental edits do NOT count as this slice's kit pass.

The only `Map.of()` / `Set.of()` call sites with arguments build TupleDomain.withColumnDomains() input and a DynamicFilter.getColumnsCovered() return. Kept per trino-main precedent to avoid subtly altering returned-type identity in SPI-passed structures.

TestDucklakeInlinedDeleteHandling.kt:209 emits a Kotlin warning `This class is not recommended for use in Kotlin. Use 'kotlin.collections.Set' instead.` — pre-existing in the port; not in scope for this kit pass (would touch the test fixture's explicit-Java-set construction).

during_correctness_wins.name: safe_sql_message
during_correctness_wins.reason: Brief rule #4: error path (`assertThat(catchThrowable {...}).isNotNull()` directly above). The original branched on null to avoid NPE; the Kotlin-idiomatic null-safe chain is equivalent and reads cleaner.
during_correctness_wins.helper: inlined — Kotlin stdlib `?.` and `.orEmpty()` are noise-free; no IdiomKit needed.

idiom_cleanup.name: kotlin_collection_factories
idiom_cleanup.reason: Empty `java.util.Map.of()` is the safe-conversion case per trino-main kit precedent (the kept_at note explicitly excludes only `Map.of(args)` with arguments). `emptyMap<String, String>()` infers via the call site, no java.util import needed.
kept_at: TestDucklakePageSourceProvider.kt:97,105,156,511 — `java.util.Map.of(priceColumn, Domain.singleValue(DOUBLE, 30.0))` etc., used as `TupleDomain.withColumnDomains` input (with arguments).

idiom_cleanup.name: drop_requireNonNull_on_kotlin_non_null_params
idiom_cleanup.reason: The file's two callers actually use Kotlin stdlib `requireNotNull(value) { "..." }` (camelCase, lazy message lambda), not the Java static import. Inspection confirmed zero in-file references to the Java `requireNonNull` symbol after the faithful port.

not_applied.name: drop_jvmstatic_from_test_companions
not_applied.rationale: Brief mandatory rule #1: KEEP existing @JvmStatic in tests. JUnit reflectively loads @BeforeAll/@AfterAll/@MethodSource targets on companion objects; the Trino testing framework (QueryRunner builders, DistributedQueryRunner) similarly reflects on static factory methods. Removing @JvmStatic risks runtime resolution failures the compiler can't catch. The trino-main rule (drop because the module is 100% Kotlin) does NOT transfer to test code.
not_applied.decision: Deliberately kept by policy. 259 sites across 42 files.

not_applied.name: collapse_optional_to_nullable_on_internal_paths
not_applied.rationale: Deferred across all prior slices (trino-main, catalog-main, catalog-test). Per brief rule #7. Trino tests use Optional<T> to interface with main-source APIs (DucklakeColumnHandle's pre-handle-migration callers, DucklakeTableHandle.snapshotId etc.) and Trino-SPI return types (ConnectorMetadata.getInfo). Flipping at test-call boundaries would require coordinated changes in the main slice already kit-applied.

not_applied.name: resource_leak_fixes_use_block
not_applied.rationale: Brief rule #2: only swap try/finally → .use {} when the test was clearly closing one Closeable, not orchestrating multi-step teardown. All inspected try/finally sites in this slice are multi-step orchestration: `try { computeActual(...) ... } finally { tryDropTable(name) }` (drops a test table after the body runs — not Closeable), executor shutdown + flag reset patterns, JdbcDucklakeCatalog teardown chains. None match the safe-conversion criterion.
not_applied.decision: Not applicable — no safe single-Closeable conversions available.

not_applied.name: locale_root_lowercase_on_arch_string
not_applied.rationale: Brief rule #3: fix only if there's a real catalog-identifier hazard. The one default-locale `lowercase()` in this slice (TestDucklakeDuckDbExecutorBackends.kt:92) reads `os.arch` (always ASCII: 'aarch64', 'x86_64', 'amd64'); the Turkish-locale hazard cannot apply. The two error-message lowercases in TestDucklakeArithmeticPushdownParity match ASCII substrings ('division by zero', 'divide by zero', '/ by zero') against thrown JDBC/SQL messages; cosmetic locale-correctness here would just be noise.
not_applied.decision: Not applicable — no catalog-identifier hazard exists.

not_applied.name: convert_handles_to_jvm_record_data_class
not_applied.rationale: Brief rule #6: skip — tests don't have Jackson-serialized handles. Confirmed: zero @JsonCreator / @JsonProperty handle types in this slice; tests construct/consume the handles defined in main sources (which were already converted to @JvmRecord data class in the trino-main slice, commit edab5b4).

not_applied.name: move_companion_helpers_to_top_level
not_applied.rationale: Inherited deferral from trino-main kit-notes. Test helpers on companion objects (e.g. createDynamicFilter, drain, varcharConst) are local to single test files and not @JvmStatic-call-pattern-blocking; converting them ripples through every call site for no measurable readability win.

shared_helpers_decision.rationale: With only 5 edits — 2 mechanical `Map.of()` → `emptyMap()`, 1 dead-import removal, 2 inlined `?.lowercase().orEmpty()` patterns — extracting a helper would add more noise than it removes. The trino-main IdiomKit.kt (lowercaseRoot, safeMessage, firstLine, firstLineOf) remains the canonical reference if a future test needs the helper. Test files now read identically to the precedent — no module-local infrastructure needed.

verification.expected: 825/0/0/0 across 70 suites (matches .ai/kotlin-port/trino-test/baseline.json and the trino-main post-kit run)
verification.last_run: 2026-06-01 — PASSED. Parsed from on-disk TEST-*.xml: suites=70 tests=825 failures=0 errors=0 skipped=0. Matches baseline exactly.
